preamble: The shift from a back fricative (velar or uvular) to the labiodental fricative [f] is a well-documented 'imitative' sound change, driven by the acoustic similarity between posterior and anterior 'grave' fricatives.

examples.note: Historical shift of the voiceless velar/uvular fricative to a labiodental fricative based on acoustic reinterpretation.
Loss of the posterior constriction in favor of a labial one sharing similar spectral properties.
Documented in specific environments where back dorsal fricatives are acoustically confused with labials.
